Document:
# ROGUE-CLASS STEALTH SHUTTLE
[color=#a4885c]Ship Size:[/color] Micro
[color=#a4885c]Recommended Crew:[/color] 1
[color=#a4885c]Power Gen Type:[/color] APU
[color=#a4885c]Expeditions:[/color] No
[color=#a4885c]IFF Console:[/color] Yes
[color=#a4885c]Restrictions:[/color] Fleet Shuttle
"Micro assault vessel with a toggle for going dark in deep space. Equipped with a 2 barrel grenade launcher."
# Tips on running NSF Rogue shuttles - The Rogue supports up to [color=#a4885c]12 [bold]kW[/bold][/color] on its shuttle APUs. It is not recommended to go above this due to the small interior space. For more general information on the power grid and other life support systems, refer to the [textlink="Power" link="Power"] guidebook article and the [textlink="Preflight Checklist" link="PreflightChecklist"/]. - There is one button in the cockpit: The button with the red and black frame fires the grenade launcer at the front of the shuttle.
- For the basics of [bold]shuttle piloting[/bold], refer to [textlink="Piloting" link="Piloting"] and [textlink="Sector Topology" link="SectorTopology"/]. - For the basics of [bold]cargo hauling[/bold], refer to [textlink="Cargo Hauling" link="CargoHauling"]. - For the basics of [bold]expedition diving[/bold], refer to [textlink="Expeditions" link="Expeditions"]. - As a Captain of a Nanotrasen vessel, you have the authority to hire, fire, demote, or promote crew members at will, refer to [textlink="Hiring" link="Hiring"] for more information.
